economic_notes: First Nation has a government business enterprise (Moosomin First Nation Economic Development Company Limited Partnership) which holds 50% interest in Western Alliance Tubulars Inc., a supplier/manufacturer for oil and gas industry that also has 25% interest in a medical mask business. Significant advances of $2,769,502 made to Western Alliance Tubulars Inc. with measurement uncertainty. COVID-19 impacted operations through increased pandemic expenditures, decreased casino-linked CDC funding, and decreased First Nations Trust funding, partially offset by ISC COVID-19 funding.
notes: Fiscal year is 2021, not 2024. Auditor's report includes an Emphasis of Matter paragraph regarding significant advances ($2,769,502) made to Western Alliance Tubulars Inc. (a 50%-owned investee of the Nation's business entity) with significant measurement uncertainty - opinion not modified. Four entities consolidated: Moosomin First Nation, Moosomin First Nation Education Council Inc., Moosomin First Nation C.M.H.C. Housing Projects, Moosomin First Nation Health Authority Inc. One GBE accounted for by modified equity method: Moosomin First Nation Economic Development Company Limited Partnership. Moosomin 1909 Surrender Trust excluded as Chief and Council do not control the Trust. CMHC replacement and operating reserves are underfunded. Prior year had a one-time $1,957,031 contribution to Saulteaux First Nation for Sewage Pumping Station recorded as other expense. total_government_transfers estimated as ISC ($11,627,119) + CMHC ($653,609) + First Nations Trust ($430,674) + Battle River Treaty 6 Health Centre ($141,745) + Battlefords Agency Tribal Chiefs ($304,716) + SITAG ($157,532) - some of these may be transfer payments rather than government transfers; isc_funding is ISC total only.
